description: SQLAlchemy base model with audit fields (id, created_at, updated_at, created_by, updated_by)
acceptance_criteria: All models inherit from BaseModel, audit fields auto-populated via SQLAlchemy events

description: User SQLAlchemy model with email, password_hash, full_name, role, active fields and constraints
acceptance_criteria: User model with unique email constraint, role enum, indexes on email and active fields

description: RequestLog model for audit trail with correlation_id, method, endpoint, status_code, response_time_ms
acceptance_criteria: RequestLog model with foreign key to User, correlation_id unique constraint

description: SQLAlchemy database configuration with async engine, session factory, and get_db dependency
acceptance_criteria: Database session management, connection pooling, async session support

description: User repository with CRUD operations: create, get_by_email, get_by_id, update, list_users
acceptance_criteria: Async repository methods, email uniqueness check, user filtering by active status

description: RequestLog repository for creating audit trail entries
acceptance_criteria: Async method to create request log entries with performance metrics

description: Pydantic schemas for auth endpoints: RegisterRequest, LoginRequest, TokenResponse
acceptance_criteria: Input validation for email format, password length, response schema with JWT token

description: Pydantic schemas for user endpoints: UserResponse, UserUpdateRequest, PasswordChangeRequest
acceptance_criteria: User profile schemas without sensitive fields, password validation for updates

description: Pydantic schemas for admin endpoints: UserListResponse, RoleUpdateRequest, StatusUpdateRequest
acceptance_criteria: Admin-specific schemas with pagination, role enum validation, status update schemas

description: Common Pydantic schemas: ErrorResponse, PaginatedResponse, HealthCheckResponse
acceptance_criteria: Standardized error format with correlation_id, pagination envelope, health check format

description: FastAPI router for authentication endpoints: POST /register, POST /login
acceptance_criteria: Register returns 201 with JWT token, login returns 200 with JWT token, proper error handling

description: FastAPI router for user endpoints: GET /me, PUT /me, PUT /me/password
acceptance_criteria: JWT-protected endpoints, user can only access own profile, password change validation

description: FastAPI router for admin endpoints: GET /users, PUT /users/{id}/role, PUT /users/{id}/status
acceptance_criteria: Admin-only endpoints with pagination, role management, user activation/deactivation

description: FastAPI router for health check endpoints: GET /live, GET /ready
acceptance_criteria: Liveness returns 200 OK, readiness checks database and circuit breaker status

description: JWT token generation, validation, password hashing, and auth dependencies
acceptance_criteria: BCrypt password hashing, JWT creation/validation, FastAPI auth dependencies for USER/ADMIN

description: Authentication business logic: user registration, login, token validation
acceptance_criteria: Email uniqueness check, password validation, BCrypt hashing, JWT generation, rate limiting

description: User management business logic: profile updates, password changes
acceptance_criteria: Profile update with email uniqueness, password change with current password verification

description: Admin operations business logic: user listing, role management, status updates
acceptance_criteria: Paginated user listing, role updates with validation, prevent self-deactivation

description: Health check business logic: liveness check, database connectivity, circuit breaker status
acceptance_criteria: Database connection test, circuit breaker status reporting, response under 100ms

description: Circuit breaker implementation with CLOSED, OPEN, HALF_OPEN states for database operations
acceptance_criteria: Configurable failure threshold, timeout handling, state management, async support

description: Custom exception classes and global exception handler
acceptance_criteria: Exception hierarchy, HTTP status mapping, global FastAPI exception handler with correlation ID

description: HTTP middleware for correlation ID generation, request logging, CORS, security headers
acceptance_criteria: UUID correlation ID per request, structured request/response logging, security headers

description: Structured logging configuration with correlation ID support and JSON formatting
acceptance_criteria: Structlog configuration, JSON output in production, correlation ID context, sensitive data filtering

description: Pydantic Settings configuration for environment variables and app configuration
acceptance_criteria: Environment-based configuration, JWT settings, database URL, CORS origins, validation

description: FastAPI application factory with middleware, routers, exception handlers, and startup/shutdown events
acceptance_criteria: FastAPI app with all routers, middleware stack, global exception handler, database initialization

description: Python dependencies: FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, structlog, passlib, PyJWT, httpx, pydantic
acceptance_criteria: All required dependencies with version pinning, compatible versions, security-focused choices

description: Alembic configuration file for database migrations
acceptance_criteria: Alembic configuration with SQLAlchemy async support, migration directory setup

description: Alembic environment configuration with async SQLAlchemy support
acceptance_criteria: Async migration support, model metadata import, database URL from config

description: Initial database migration creating users and request_logs tables
acceptance_criteria: Creates users table with constraints, indexes, and request_logs table with foreign keys
